post.description:
Called periodically by a verification agent to report capacity and refresh last-seen.
Authentication uses the agent's raw token (NOT a user JWT) — the same token returned at create/rotate.
Called by a verification agent. Returns the oldest PENDING verification that fits the agent's disk budget, or 204 if nothing fits.

get.description: Streams the decrypted backup file to the agent that owns this RUNNING verification. Same bytes the user-facing /backups/{id}/download endpoint returns.
get.summary: Stream the backup file for a verification

post.description: Agent reports COMPLETED (with stats) or FAILED. CAS-guarded: stale or foreign reports get 410.

post.description: Transfer a database from one workspace to another. Can transfer to a new storage or transfer with the existing storage. Can also specify target notifiers from the target workspace.

parameters.description: Transfer request with targetWorkspaceId, storage options (targetStorageId or isTransferWithStorage), and optional targetNotifierIds
parameters.name: request

400.description: Invalid request, target storage/notifier not in target workspace, or transfer failed

get.summary: Get physical backup configuration by database ID

post.description: Save or update physical backup configuration for a database. Encryption can be set to NONE (no encryption) or ENCRYPTED (AES-256-GCM encryption).
Save or update backup configuration for a database. Encryption can be set to NONE (no encryption) or ENCRYPTED (AES-256-GCM encryption).

parameters.description: Backup configuration data (encryption field: NONE or ENCRYPTED)
parameters.name: request

200.description: Returns the saved backup configuration including encryption settings

400.description: Invalid encryption value or other validation errors

Check if a storage is currently being used by any backup configuration

get.description: Get paginated backups for the specified database with optional filters

parameters.description: Filter by backup status (can be repeated)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Filter backups created before this date (RFC3339)
parameters.name: beforeDate

post.description: Create a new backup for the specified database

delete.description: Deletes a backup. A FULL removes its whole chain (incrementals + WAL); an incremental removes it and its descendant incrementals (WAL kept); a WAL segment removes it and the later WAL up to the next FULL/incremental. A running backup in the removed set is cancelled first.
delete.summary: Delete a physical backup and its dependent cascade

parameters.description: Backup ID (FULL, incremental or WAL segment)
parameters.name: backupId

post.description: Stops a FULL or incremental backup that is currently running and releases its in-flight claim. Returns 400 if the backup is not in progress.
Issues a single-use token authorizing a per-backup restore stream: a FULL restores just itself; an incremental restores its FULL plus its incremental ancestors. No WAL replay is involved.
post.summary: Issue a restore-stream token for a specific backup

get.description: Returns a page of physical backups — FULLs, incrementals and committed WAL segments — as one flat list newest-first, with the database's total on-disk usage and the total count for pagination. The frontend filters by type.
get.summary: List physical backups for a database

parameters.description: Filter by backup type - repeatable, matches any
parameters.name: type

parameters.description: Filter by status - repeatable, matches any (e.g. COMPLETED, IN_PROGRESS)
parameters.name: status

parameters.description: Keep only backups created before this date (RFC3339)
parameters.name: beforeDate

post.description: Issues a single-use, short-TTL token that authorizes the agent-less restore stream. Omit targetTime to restore to the latest available point.
Requests an out-of-cadence backup, honored on the scheduler's next tick. type=full forces a new full; type=incremental extends the current chain (409 if none); type=auto lets the scheduler pick.

get.description: Returns a POSIX sh script that takes a restore-stream URL (with its token) or a local bundle path, decompresses the backup blobs and WAL with the host's zstd up front, runs pg_combinebackup and wires up recovery with a plain-cp restore_command so the started cluster needs no extra tools. Pass --combine-image <postgres-image> to run pg_combinebackup in a container when the host lacks the PostgreSQL tools. It carries no secrets, so it needs no auth.

Resolves the restore set for the token's database + target and streams an artifact-only tar of the stored, still-compressed bytes (full/base.tar.zst, incr-N/base.tar.zst, each with a backup_manifest, plus wal/ and MANIFEST.sha256). curl '<url>' | tar -x, then run the reconstruction command shown in the UI (decompress the blobs, pg_combinebackup + recovery settings). Public: authorized by the ?token= query param, not Bearer.
get.summary: Stream a ready-to-restore directory as a tar

post.description: Cancel a backup that is currently in progress
Generate a token for downloading a backup file (valid for 5 minutes)

get.description:
Download the backup file for the specified backup using a download token.

**Download Concurrency Control:**
- Only one download per user is allowed at a time
- If a download is already in progress, returns 409 Conflict
- Downloads are tracked using cache with 5-second TTL and 3-second heartbeat
- Browser cancellations automatically release the download lock
- Server crashes are handled via automatic cache expiry (5 seconds)

get.description: Get backup verification configuration for a specific database. Lazily creates a disabled default on first read.

put.description: Create or update backup verification configuration for a database. Verification cannot be enabled for WAL-based PostgreSQL databases.

get.description: List all live verification agents (ADMIN only).

post.description: Create a new verification-worker agent (ADMIN only). The raw token is returned exactly once.

get.description: Returns the count of live verification agents. Available to any authenticated user so the UI can decide whether to render verification-related actions. No agent identifiers or tokens are exposed.

delete.description: Soft-delete a verification agent (ADMIN only). The agent's token is preserved so a still-running worker receives a 404 on its next heartbeat rather than a 401.

post.description: Mint a new token for the agent and invalidate the old one (ADMIN only). The raw token is returned exactly once.

get.description: Returns verifications for the given database, newest first. Per-table stats are NOT included — use GetByID for that.

post.description: Trigger a one-off verification of the chosen backup. Returns 400 if a manual verification is already pending or running for the same database — cancel it first.

get.description: Returns one verification row with its per-table stats preloaded.

post.description: Marks a PENDING or RUNNING verification as CANCELED. For RUNNING rows the agent stops on its next heartbeat. Returns 400 if the verification is already terminal.
post.summary: Cancel a pending or running verification

timescaledbVersion.description:
TimescaledbVersion is the source's timescaledb extension version captured at backup
time, or "" when the source has no timescaledb. Non-empty marks the backup as needing
the TimescaleDB restore procedure, and the verification agent uses the exact version to
pull a matching engine image (pg_restore cannot cross extension versions).

targetTime.description:
TargetTime is the PITR target; omit for a restore to the latest available
point.

total.description:
Total is the count of all backups (every type) for the database, for
driving pagination; Limit/Offset echo the page that was served.

failMessage.description: FailMessage is the human-readable failure detail, ERROR / CHAIN_BROKEN rows only.

walFilename.description: WalFilename is the bare PG segment name, WAL rows only.

lastBackupTime.description:
these fields are not reliable, but
they are used for pretty UI

workspaceId.description:
WorkspaceID can be null when a database is created via restore operation
outside the context of any workspace

walSegmentSizeBytes.description: WalSegmentSizeBytes captures the source cluster's wal_segment_size at first connect.

isAllowExternalRegistrations.description: means that any user can register via sign up form without invitation

isAllowMemberInvitations.description: means that any user with role MEMBER can invite other users

isMemberAllowedToCreateWorkspaces.description: means that any user with role MEMBER can create their own workspaces

currentVerificationIds.description:
What the agent thinks it's running. Server returns the
subset to abort if some information is outdated

abortVerificationIds.description:
IDs that vanished, were flipped to CANCELED or are no longer owned
by this agent. So it needs to drop them.

userRole.description: User's role in this workspace (populated when fetching for specific user)
